passions_detected.false_judgement: That others' success reveals personal deprivation — 'coveting is imagining the pleasure they must feel'
That cars and resourcefulness demonstration are genuine goods
That rockstar fantasy/creative recognition represents a lost genuine good

causal_observations.description: Envy reframed: 'pivoting envy to curiosity' — learning to withhold assent from the impression that others' success = personal lack. Breaking down their virtues is 'the real gift of their success'.
Social media as false impression generator — 'reduce source of negative emotions' but with wisdom not to quit impulsively.

value_observations.declared_as: the right mindset focused on four virtues: Courage (take action), Justice (do the right thing), Temperance (do it consistently), Wisdom (calculated with resources)
things I have that I once craved
value_observations.observed_as: still attached to the 'exact amount in the exact way at the exact time' — working to let go

value_observations.declared_as: 'there is no good or bad in nature just true or false'
value_observations.observed_as: strongest philosophical articulation in the journal

virtue_observations.evidence: 'There is no good or bad in nature just true or false everything else is just my or their perspective'. This is the strongest philosophical formulation in the journal — genuine practical wisdom.
Music metaphor for contentment — 'things and pleasures need to be culled down to small morsels of savoury and shareable treats'
Back pain reframed: 'contentment will come once I have done all I can to improve the situation. Lose weight, exercise, become a man I have never been.'

key_passages.summary: Contentment formula: Courage (take action), Justice (do the right thing), Temperance (do it consistently), Wisdom (calculated with resources available)
Freedom through truth: 'there is no good or bad in nature just true or false — act not on the perceptions but on the true/false that is the root cause'
Music metaphor for contentment — 'things and pleasures need to be culled down to small morsels of savoury and shareable treats' — instead of search for more, attention to resourcefulness
